Assassin.GiftOfVenomDesc: Roll the Poison Die and add it to the attack and damage die. On a result of 3 or higher the target must make a Fortitude save against the total attack roll or suffer a poison effect (roll d7, modified by Luck). If the assassin chooses not to fight with poison, the Poison Die is added to attack and damage only, with no extra effect and no save.
Assassin.AssassinationsDesc: When striking a target completely unawares (not merely surprised), the assassin deals normal damage; on a successful Poison Die any victim of equal or lesser level/HD must make a Fortitude save against the to-hit roll or die instantly.
Assassin.HiddenWeaponDesc: The assassin carries one concealed weapon that can be drawn and used in one round. It deals 1d4 damage (other modifiers still apply).
Assassin.MasterOfPoisonDesc: Requires no Handle Poison roll to administer poisons, and is immune to his or her own poisons. On a fumble, a poisoned weapon may affect allies.
Assassin.ThievesCantDesc: Fluent in all Thieves' Cants, plus the secret assassins' tongue. Uses the thief's Backstab, Sneak Silently, Hide in Shadows and Disguise Self skills.
Assassin.Poison1: Slowed: opponent at half movement for d20 rounds.
Assassin.Poison2: Dazed: mildly stunned; all skills and attacks at -4.
Assassin.Poison3: Weakened: opponent can move but not attack, and may use no Strength-based skills.
Assassin.Poison4: Inflicts d7 extra hp damage; opponent foams at mouth and wound.
Assassin.Poison5: Opponent immobilized by nausea and dizziness for d14 rounds; cannot attack or move.
Assassin.Poison6: Shock: opponent completely stunned for 1d4 rounds and suffers an additional 1d7 damage.
Assassin.Credit: Assassin by Julian Bernick — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2015 Collection, Volume 1: Men & Magic.
Dervish.HolyRiteDesc: Each order has a sacred physical discipline (self-flagellation, martial practice, sacred dance, musical performance, etc.). Choose one as your Holy Rite. A dervish may use the Deed Die only with the chosen weapon of the order, and shuns missile weapons.
Dervish.LayOnHandsDesc: May Lay on Hands as a cleric, once per day per level, always as if the target shares the dervish's alignment. May also heal him- or herself by spending an hour performing a Holy Rite (1d6 damage or 1 point of ability damage per level).
Dervish.ThievingSkillsDesc: Choose two: Sneak Silently, Hide in Shadows, Climb Sheer Surfaces, Pick Lock, Find Trap, Read Languages, Handle Poison, Cast Spell from Scroll. Apply the Deed Die to those rolls.
Dervish.TrackingDesc: Add the Deed Die to any roll to track a target of divine retribution (Intelligence for physical tracks, Personality for inquiries). Success grants a boon; failure means the quest must continue.
Dervish.SurvivalDesc: Add the Deed Die to attempts to find shelter, start fires, and locate food and water, even in the most inhospitable climes.
Dervish.AsceticismDesc: Keeps only utilitarian possessions and gives away surplus wealth. A dervish who covets wealth loses 1 Luck per day until the surplus is disposed of (not by giving it to a party member).
Dervish.LanguagesDesc: Renowned travelers, dervishes learn 1d3 additional languages per level beyond those granted by Intelligence.
Dervish.Credit: Dervish by Edgar Johnson — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2015 Collection, Volume 1: Men & Magic.
Luchador.MasksOfPowerDesc: Every Luchador wears a mask, the source of their strength. Removing it denies all class abilities; a forcefully removed mask stuns the Luchador for 1d5 rounds. Rebuilding a destroyed mask takes 2d3 days and 10d6 gp.
Luchador.UnarmedCombatDesc: Unarmed attacks do full normal damage by level. May strike with both hands (Two-Weapon Fighting). Reduces large-monster grapple bonus by level (min 0) and the ally-strike chance by 5% per level. Critical hits while unarmed roll on the Unarmed (Lucha) Crit Table.
Luchador.Tecnicos: Tecnicos (Lawful): add the Lucha die to unarmed attack and damage and to Mighty Deeds of Wrestling; AC increases by 1/2 the rolled value (round up) until the next action; may add the die to sway lawful people.
Luchador.Rudos: Rudos (Chaotic): add the Lucha die to unarmed attack and damage and to Mighty Deeds of Wrestling; unarmed critical threat range increases by 1/2 the rolled value (round down) until the next action; may add the die to bully chaotic people.
Luchador.Credit: Luchador by Reid “Reidzilla” San Filippo — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2015 Collection, Volume 1: Men & Magic.
SwordMonger.TenetOfTheBladeDesc: Sword Mongers may use only swords (any type). Using any other weapon imposes -1d on the dice chain and is viewed as cowardly.
SwordMonger.BladeDeedDesc: Declared before an attack roll; the Deed Die is added to attack and damage. A successful Deed (3+) adds no extra damage but can produce an additional effect (blinding blood, cut straps lowering AC, etc.) provided the sword is integral to the deed.
SwordMonger.BladeLuckDesc: Add the Luck modifier to all attacks made with a sword. This bonus is fixed and never changes throughout the Sword Monger's life.
SwordMonger.InitiativeDesc: Adds class level to initiative rolls.
SwordMonger.ImmortalityDesc: Unless decapitated (the silver cord severed), the Sword Monger does not suffer true death and automatically passes recover-the-body checks. Random ability loss applies, and Personality is reduced by 1 for battle scars; once Personality is negative, the penalty may be used as a bonus for threats and intimidation.
SwordMonger.SenseSiblingDesc: Senses others of their kind within 100 miles × level, and may track them by spending one round concentrating.
SwordMonger.CuttingTheCordDesc: Slaying another Sword Monger (assumed when the loser reaches 0 hp) grants 20% of the loser's total hit points (rounded down) and a 25% increase to all stats. Every fourth such victory grants +1 to all physical stats.
SwordMonger.Credit: Sword Monger by David Baity — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2015 Collection, Volume 1: Men & Magic.
MartialGrandmaster.WeaponTrainingDesc: Begins with proficiency in four melee weapons. Becomes proficient with any newly picked-up weapon after 1d6 rounds of real combat (until then, -1d to attack and damage; the judge may reduce this to -1 for similar weapons). Rarely wears armor — damage rolls take the armor's check penalty.
MartialGrandmaster.InitiativeAbilityDesc: Initiative is always the top result of the second-highest Action Die plus level (e.g. 15 at 1st level, 20 at 4th). Cannot burn Luck to raise it unless also rolling for initiative normally.
MartialGrandmaster.CombatLuckDesc: While the party rolls initiative, the grandmaster instead rolls 1d6 + level for Combat Luck points, spent during that battle to boost attack rolls, damage, or saves (leaving the Luck attribute untouched). Unused points vanish at the end of combat.
MartialGrandmaster.CombatSurvivorDesc: If reduced to 0 hp in combat, before any other restorative mechanic, roll 1d10 minus current Luck modifier; if the result is at or below current Combat Luck points, the grandmaster survives at 1 hp. Not usable if a crit specifies death.
MartialGrandmaster.AsAgileAsTheWindDesc: Movement speed 40'. Gains +1d on all agility- and athletics-type checks; a critical success grants a judge-determined bonus (an extra move or attack is a good default).
MartialGrandmaster.LikeHittingAThoughtDe: If positive, the Agility modifier applies doubled to AC and Reflex saves (a negative Agility modifier applies to neither). May burn an Action Die for +1d4 AC until next turn (+1d6 modified by Luck if the highest die is burned), or burn 2 Combat Luck for +1 AC for the rest of the fight.
MartialGrandmaster.MyBodyIsAWeaponDesc: Unarmed attacks deal 1d5 + level damage. If all Action Dice are used for unarmed attacks and two or more hit in a round, add level again (once) to the total, distributable among targets.
MartialGrandmaster.AlwaysMovingDesc: With at least 1 unused Combat Luck point, the grandmaster may freely split movement and attacks across a round as long as movement and Action Dice remain.
MartialGrandmaster.CriticalPrecisionDesc: Whatever die is rolled for an attack, rolling its highest number scores both a hit and a Critical Hit.
MartialGrandmaster.KnockProne: Knock Prone: target is knocked down; the grandmaster's level is a penalty on the target's next-round attacks. (2 Combat Luck)
MartialGrandmaster.Impair: Impair: target's attacks suffer a penalty equal to half the grandmaster's level (rounded up) +1, fading 1 point per round. (1 Combat Luck)
MartialGrandmaster.Stun: Stun: target loses its highest Action Die next turn; if the attack beats AC by 5+, it loses its next 1d3 Action Dice. (3 Combat Luck)
MartialGrandmaster.Guard: Guard: +3 AC versus the foe struck until next round, extendable to adjacent allies; ends if the grandmaster becomes mobility-impaired. (2 Combat Luck)
MartialGrandmaster.Credit: Martial Grandmaster by bygrinstow (art by Matt Sutton) — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 7: Character Classes. Originally inspired by the statblock in the 2015 Almanac, Volume 1.
Peasant.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained only in the weapons of their occupation, plus implements regularly used by that profession (e.g. a farmer's pitchfork, shovel, or slaughtering knife).
Peasant.ExpertDesc: Add a bonus equal to peasant level to skill checks related to the character's occupation.
Peasant.LuckDesc: Add the Luck modifier to checks made in the character's profession or hobby.
Peasant.HobbyDesc: From 2nd level, the peasant adopts a hobby: pick one other occupation and make checks in it using the Hobby Die (from the table) instead of the normal untrained d10.
Peasant.LifeOfAdventureDesc: A peasant may abandon peasant life to become an adventurer: lose all but 10 XP and most level benefits (reverting to a 0-level peasant at creation hp), then choose a 1st-level class (humans) or become a professional Elf/Dwarf/Halfling. Keeps the hobby die, trained abilities, and accumulated property.
Peasant.Credit: Peasant by Matthew Carr (art by Colin Mills) — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 7: Character Classes.
Barbearian.NaturalWeaponsDesc: As an action, either two claw attacks (1d4 each) or one bite (1d6). Cannot grasp or use manufactured weapons. Against a pinned, grappled foe, may bite at +2 to hit and then rend with claws for +1d5 (at 5th level: +4 to hit, 1d8 bite, 1d10 rend).
Barbearian.ThickSkinDesc: Natural AC 12 at 1st level, +1 every even level to a maximum of 17 at 10th. Cannot wear armor; may wear a necklace but no other magic items.
Barbearian.BerzerkerRageDesc: A number of times per day (see table), the barbearian may rage as an action; the rage lasts the whole combat and grants +1d to all die rolls.
Barbearian.BearHugDesc: Extra Strength-check bonus when grappling, on top of size bonuses: +4 vs a smaller creature, +2 vs equal size, none vs larger.
Barbearian.MissileSwatDesc: If aware of the attack, may attempt a Reflex save (DC = the missile's attack roll) to swat an incoming arrow or missile out of the air.
Barbearian.ClimberDesc: Climbs trees with no check (other wooden structures at the judge's discretion). Climb speed 20'.
Barbearian.LargeAndSlowDesc: An adult barbearian is a Large creature (twice human size) with a base movement of 20'. Speaks the bear tongue (Appendix L).
Barbearian.Credit: Barbearian by Randy Andrews (art by Jonathan Byrne) — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 8.
HeavenlyHitman.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained in blackjack, blowgun, crossbow, dagger, dart, garrote, longsword, short sword, sling, and staff. Restricted to leather armor.
HeavenlyHitman.MagicDesc: Casts spells as a cleric (Personality is the spellcasting ability). Uses the Spells tab.
HeavenlyHitman.DivineWrathDesc: In place of a cleric's healing, on a successful backstab the hitman may make a Channel Harm check (1d20 + level + Personality) and add the table's damage to the attack. See the Channel Harm table.
HeavenlyHitman.ThievingSkillsDesc: Uses the thief skill block (backstab, sneak silently, hide in shadows, climb sheer surfaces, pick locks, find/disable traps, forge documents, handle poison).
HeavenlyHitman.LuckDesc: Add the Luck modifier to backstab damage.
HeavenlyHitman.Credit: Heavenly Hitman by Ed Kabara — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 8: Character Class — Styles & Rules.
Human.HighlySkilledDesc: Rolls 1d14 for untrained skill checks and 1d24 for trained skill checks.
Human.MasterOfDiplomacyDesc: Spend 1 Personality point to force a target to make a Will save (DC 8 + Per mod + class level) or be persuaded; extra Personality raises the DC or affects more targets.
Human.InfiniteVarietyDesc: At 1st level choose one: Warrior (Mighty Deed of Arms as a 1st-level fighter), Wizard (two random wizard spells), Cleric (two random cleric spells), or Thief (thief skills) — all frozen at 1st-level ability and never improving.
Human.HumanHubrisDesc: May voluntarily fail any Will save to regain one spent point of Personality or Luck, up to the starting maximum.
Human.LuckDesc: Applies the Luck modifier to any skill check, trained or untrained.
Human.LanguagesDesc: Knows Common, the predominant race's language, and one random Appendix L language at 1st level.
Human.Credit: Human by Dieter Zimmerman — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 7: Character Classes.
FowlSummoner.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained in daggers, short swords, and staffs; proficient at kicking and scratching; may wear armor only if it has a fowl aesthetic.
FowlSummoner.SpellcastingDesc: A limited wizard caster whose spells have morphed toward birds — lower arcane potential but sturdier, with greater chaos on misfires. Personality is the spellcasting ability. Starts with Fowl Summoning, Bird Strike/Bird Storm, Feather Fall, and Find Familiar (bird only).
FowlSummoner.SummoningDesc: Summons birds from a custom bestiary (the "Bird Book") via long spell-check result tables; summons may not benefit bystanders.
FowlSummoner.PatronDesc: May only seek a Patron of birds, and ideally begins as a 0-level with a feathered companion.
FowlSummoner.PersonalityLossDesc: As the summoner cares more for birds than people, Personality drops by an increasing penalty at even levels (-1 at 2nd up to -5 at 10th).
FowlSummoner.PeckingOrderDesc: Only one Fowl Summoner is allowed per party.
FowlSummoner.CasterNote: Spells are managed on the Spells tab. Known spells rise from 4 (level 1) to 12 (level 10); max spell level from 1 to 5.
FowlSummoner.Credit: Fowl Summoner — words and art by Clayton Williams — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 7: Character Classes.
TarantinoElf.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained in crossbow, longsword, short sword, blackjack, blowgun, dagger, and garrote; deals backstab-style extra damage on the first attack (regained on an Agility check vs DC 20 − level).
TarantinoElf.SpellcastingDesc: Casts wizard spells as an elf (Intelligence). Spellburn scatters: every living being within 10' must make a Will save (DC 10 + points) or lose half the spellburn as hp; spellburn cannot be healed without the bonded Pocketwatch.
TarantinoElf.CatchPhraseDesc: A showy quote grants an opposed Personality check at +1d against up to level-many enemies; losers lose their next Action Die to fear or confusion.
TarantinoElf.IronDesc: Vulnerable to iron like common elves, but wielding an iron weapon grants +1d to attack and damage while losing 1 hp/round (healed after dropping it).
TarantinoElf.UltraViolence: A Bit of the Old Ultra-Violence
TarantinoElf.UltraViolenceDesc: On a critical hit, roll the crit die plus an extra crit die one step lower (both Luck-modified) as two crits; matching results triple that crit. Threat range widens with level (20 → 19-20 → 18-20). Uses the Monster crit chart.
TarantinoElf.LegendaryMoveDesc: Declare a sworn nemesis per adventure; the once-per-target Legendary Move gains +1d, deals max damage, and crits on a hit. Once per career, may revive a fallen ally who failed Roll the Body.
TarantinoElf.CasterNote: Wizard spells are managed on the Spells tab. Max known spells rise from 2 (level 1) to 12 (level 10); max spell level from 1 to 5.
TarantinoElf.Credit: Tarantino Elf — compiled by Jeremiah Edward Sprague, Mihailo Tešić, Ari-Matti Piippo & bygrinstow; art by Cheese Hasselberger — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 7.
PriestOldFather.Abilities: Priest of the Old Father Abilities
PriestOldFather.MagicDesc: Casts spells as a cleric (Personality is the spellcasting ability).
PriestOldFather.LayOnHandsDesc: As a cleric, except elves are always healed as the same alignment and all other races as the opposed alignment.
PriestOldFather.PatronsDesc: Like a wizard, automatically gains patron bond and invoke patron at 1st level, dedicated to the King of Elfland.
PriestOldFather.LuckDesc: Adds the Luck modifier to invoke patron and patron bond checks.
PriestOldFather.HeightenedSensesDesc: +4 to detect secret doors, and a free check when passing within 10 feet of one.
PriestOldFather.ElfTraitsDesc: Immune to magical sleep and paralysis; 60' infravision; iron sensitivity (no iron armor or weapons; 1 hp/day damage from prolonged contact).
PriestOldFather.LevelTable: Table POF-1: Priest of the Old Father
PriestOldFather.CasterNote: Cleric spells are managed on the Spells tab. Known spells rise from 3 (level 1) to 14 (level 10); max spell level from 1 to 5.
PriestOldFather.Credit: Priest of the Old Father by Ed Kabara — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 8.
ArcaneWarrior.WeaponTrainingDesc: May use any weapon (one hand must be free to cast) and wear any affordable armor with no spellcasting interference.
ArcaneWarrior.MagicDieAbilityDesc: Like a warrior's deed die, an extra Magic Die is rolled on attacks and added to both attack and damage of weapons or spells. Add the Luck modifier to all Magic Die rolls.
ArcaneWarrior.MagicOrMightDesc: Before an attack, choose to cast a wizard spell (attack must hit, Magic Die ≥ 2 + spell level, then roll Magic Die + action die + Intelligence for the effect) or perform a Mighty Deed using the Magic Die as the Deed Die.
ArcaneWarrior.CasterLevelDesc: The arcane warrior's caster level equals their class level. Knows the first three random wizard spells at 1st level.
ArcaneWarrior.LuckDesc: Adds the Luck modifier to all Magic Die rolls.
ArcaneWarrior.CasterNote: Wizard spells are managed on the Spells tab. Known spells rise from 3 (level 1) to 7 (level 10); max spell level from 1 to 5.
ArcaneWarrior.Credit: Arcane Warrior by Ed Kabara — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 8: Character Class — Styles & Rules.
MysticArcanist.PowersDesc: Gains all powers of a cleric (but not the turn ability) and casts wizard spells. Both divine and arcane spells use Intelligence.
MysticArcanist.PatronRestrictionDesc: May only ever have a patron that is a greater devil, greater fiend, or a god — the sources that grant the mystic arcanist's power.
MysticArcanist.DisapprovalCorruptionDesc: When the mystic arcanist gains disapproval or corruption from one tradition, they also suffer the other (arcane corruption brings disapproval; disapproval brings a known wizard spell's corruption).
MysticArcanist.LuckDesc: Adds the Luck score to all spellcasting checks, divine and arcane alike.
MysticArcanist.WeaponTrainingDesc: Proficient with all armor and weapons a cleric may use.
MysticArcanist.CasterNote: Wizard and cleric spells are managed on their respective Spells tabs (see Table MA-2 in the source for per-level spell counts).
MysticArcanist.Credit: Mystic Arcanist by Ed Kabara — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 8: Character Class — Styles & Rules.
SpellThief.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained in the blackjack, blowgun, crossbow, dagger, dart, garrote, longsword, short sword, sling, and staff. May wear only leather armor.
SpellThief.ThievingSkillsDesc: Picks locks, finds and disables traps, sneaks silently, hides in shadows, climbs sheer surfaces, forges documents, picks pockets, and handles poison as a thief, using the thief skill table.
SpellThief.MagicDesc: Casts wizard spells. Knows 2 spells at 1st level; to cast, roll 1d20 + Intelligence modifier + Magic Die.
SpellThief.StealSpellDesc: When targeted by a spell, may attempt to negate and learn it: roll 1d20 + Intelligence modifier + spell thief bonus + Magic Die. On a success the spell is negated and learned in addition to known spells; on a failure the spell affects the thief as normal.
SpellThief.LuckDesc: The Luck modifier applies to rolls to steal spells.
SpellThief.CasterNote: Wizard spells are managed on the Spells tab. Known spells rise from 3 (level 1) to 7 (level 10); max spell level from 1 to 5. The spell-thief bonus to steal/negate spells rises from +3 (level 1) to +15 (level 10).
SpellThief.Credit: Spell Thief by Ed Kabara — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 8: Character Class — Styles & Rules.
RuneSage.WeaponTrainingDesc: Always a dwarf. Trained in the battleaxe, club, dagger, handaxe, longsword, mace, short sword, spear, two-handed sword, and warhammer, plus the crossbow, javelin, shortbow, and sling. Wears the heaviest armor they can afford.
RuneSage.MagicDesc: Casts wizard spells. Knows 2 spells at 1st level; to cast, roll 1d20 + Intelligence modifier + Magic Die (may spellburn as a wizard). A cast spell is carved into an object over 1 minute per spell level, using the result rolled at casting, and persists for a number of days equal to the spell level.
RuneSage.RuneDesc: A carved rune may be used by anyone. On their turn a character may trigger a rune as their action; anyone other than its creator must spellburn at least 1 point (cutting themselves to fuel it with blood) to fire it, and may spellburn extra points to raise the previously rolled result. A rune sage may have only as many runes carved at once as their Rune Cast number; casting a new spell while at the limit immediately triggers the oldest rune.
RuneSage.SwordAndStoneDesc: When the rune sage's attack (magic) die comes up 3 or better, they may trigger one previously carved rune as part of that attack — once per turn.
RuneSage.DwarfTraitsDesc: Infravision to 60'. Base movement speed 20'. Automatically knows Dwarven at 1st level.
RuneSage.LuckDesc: The Luck modifier applies to the rune sage's Armor Class.
RuneSage.CasterNote: Wizard spells are managed on the Spells tab. Known spells rise from 2 (level 1) to 7 (level 10); max spell level from 1 to 5; runes carved at once (Rune Cast) from 2 to 6. Spells are cast as carved runes — see the class writeup.
RuneSage.Credit: Rune Sage by Ed Kabara — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2020 Collection, Volume 8: Character Class — Styles & Rules.
HotDogSuit.ArmorWeaponsDesc: Trained in one randomly determined weapon plus their 0-level weapon. No armor training beyond the hot-dog suit itself.
HotDogSuit.BaffleDesc: While wearing the suit, gains +2d6 AC at the start of combat — no one can make sense of it. On a 1-in-12, low-Intelligence creatures are too freaked out to attack and keep at least 20' away.
HotDogSuit.PinkPaperFlyersDesc: Telekinetic control (range 30') over the Mr. Peppy's Sandwich Shack flyers that came through the portal with them (roll 1d30+1d20+1d10+1d5+1 for the count). Used as weapons they deal 1d3 damage and slip into armor gaps; each flyer survives 1d3 strikes before being useless.
HotDogSuit.IgnoredOverlookedDesc: Everyone overlooks them, so they overhear things others would pay to learn. Once per session, roll under Luck by 4 points to request a useful tidbit from the Judge.
HotDogSuit.ElementalInteractionsDesc: The suit is flame-retardant and non-conductive: fire and electrical damage drop two steps down the dice chain. It also runs hot — a help in cold environments, a hindrance in hot ones.
HotDogSuit.LuckyBastichDesc: One Lucky Bastich: regains spent Luck at a rate of current level every 25 hours. Luckier Bastich: spent Luck counts double. More Luckier Bastich: burn Luck to reduce an attacking foe's attack, damage, spellcheck, or opposed roll by 1d3 each. Enough With The Luck: gains 1d(level) Luck on each new level, to a maximum of 18.
HotDogSuit.AdvancementDesc: After four levels of Hot-Dog Suit, may take levels in another class the Judge allows; uses the new class's action dice and crit table but adds the Hot-Dog Suit attack bonus and saves. Ignored & Overlooked and Luckier Bastich carry over, as do any remaining Pink Paper Flyers.
HotDogSuit.Credit: Hot-Dog Suit — photo posted by J. Kitchen, said by Kevin Heuer, written up by bygrinstow — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2016 Collection, Volume 1: Men, Magic & Drink.
Barbarian.DeedDieAbilityDesc: Like a warrior, rolls a deed die added to both attack and damage (a d3 at 1st level, advancing per the table). At higher levels the barbarian adds both a die and a fixed bonus. Rolled anew with each attack.
Barbarian.MightyDeedsDesc: Performs Mighty Deeds of Arms exactly as a warrior does.
Barbarian.LoinclothDesc: While wearing no armor (or nothing heavier than hide), adds the Stamina modifier to AC.
Barbarian.AnimalInstinctsDesc: Adds the initial Luck modifier (minimum +1) to all perception and surprise checks, may check even when distracted, and can spend 1 Luck point to turn a failed surprise check into a success.
Barbarian.SavageSkillsAbilityDesc: For animal-grace tasks (jumping chasms, climbing, stalking prey, tracking, finding shelter, hunting), roll 1d20 + the appropriate ability modifier + the Savage Skills bonus from the table.
Barbarian.ArmedWithAnythingDesc: Treated as trained with any hard object as an improvised weapon (small 1d4, medium 1d6, large 1d8, two-handed 1d10); any crit or fumble breaks it.
Barbarian.SmellSorceryAbilityDesc: Once per hour, sense magic as the Detect Magic spell by rolling the die from the table + level + Luck modifier — without suffering corruption, gaining disapproval, or spellburning.
Barbarian.SuperstitionsDesc: Rejects magic: carries only a few practical magic items (each extra item is a cumulative -1 Luck penalty), and makes a saving throw against every spell cast on them — even beneficial ones, including healing; a successful save negates the spell.
Barbarian.PrimitiveDesc: Illiterate and ill at ease with civilization; interacting meaningfully with civilized concepts or devices may call for an Intelligence check (Judge's discretion).
Barbarian.Credit: Barbarian by Diogo Nogueira — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2016 Collection, Volume 1: Men, Magic & Drink.
HalflingHucker.WeaponTrainingDesc: Prefers a small thrown weapon in each hand. Trained in the boomerang, dagger, handaxe, javelin, short sword, and rocks. Huckers usually wear armor.
HalflingHucker.TwoFistedThrowerDesc: Uses the standard halfling two-weapon fighting rules, but only with small thrown weapons.
HalflingHucker.HalflingTraitsDesc: Has the standard halfling's Infravision, language bonuses, Small Size, and Slow (20') speed, and uses the Halfling class table (Table 1-18) for attack, crit, saves, action dice, and Sneak & Hide.
HalflingHucker.LuckyTosserDesc: May spend a point of Luck to add a Luck Die to the attack and damage rolls of all thrown weapons for the round (even larger weapons such as spears). Regenerates Luck daily as a halfling.
HalflingHucker.PrizewinnerDesc: Carries a prizewinning hucking stone — both lucky charm and Achilles' heel — adding the Luck bonus (minimum +1) to thrown attack and damage. Losing the Prizewinner suspends Lucky Tosser for a day until a replacement is acquired or a lesser stone is learned.
HalflingHucker.Credit: Halfling Hucker by R.S. Tilton — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2016 Collection, Volume 1: Men, Magic & Drink.
TechnoNecromancer.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained in the hammer, laser sword, longsword, mace, and short sword in melee, and the laser pistol and shortbow at range. May wear any armor, but the check penalty affects spellcasting. Always chaotic — losing chaotic alignment suspends all spellcasting until it reverts.
TechnoNecromancer.TechnoMagicalAccessory: At 1st level and each new level, a tinkering project yields a usable techno-magical accessory (roll d12+1 at 1st level, then d12+caster level, on Table TN-3). Each is AC 15 with 5 hp and cannot be repaired once destroyed.
TechnoNecromancer.MagicDesc: Innate spellcaster needing no spellbook, drawing on a bespoke necromancy list (Table TN-2). Spell check is the action die + Caster Level + Intelligence modifier; may spellburn like a wizard, but recovers spellburn only via a 4-hour necromantic blood ritual (regain 1d4 points per HD of a creature killed, once per day).
TechnoNecromancer.CyberanimateDeadDesc: Animates corpses as mechano-constructs (Table TN-4) with a spell check; treated as a level-2 spell that can be lost for the day and recalled with spellburn. The constructs are not true undead and are unaffected by anti-undead magic.
TechnoNecromancer.LuckDesc: At 1st level, permanently applies the Luck modifier either to the Will save bonus or to Cyberanimate Dead spell checks.
TechnoNecromancer.LanguagesDesc: Knows Common, plus one additional language per point of positive Intelligence modifier.
TechnoNecromancer.CasterNote: Spells are managed on the Spells tab and drawn from the techno-necromancer list (Table TN-2). Spells known rise from 2 (level 1) to 13 (level 10); max spell level from 1 to 5.
TechnoNecromancer.Credit: The Techno-necromancer by Tim Callahan — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2016 Collection, Volume 6: Crawljammer & Interdimensional Travel.
DwarfSapper.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained in the blackjack, blowgun, crossbow, dagger, garrote, short sword, hammer, mace, handaxe, club, pick, sling, and shortbow. May wear any armor but generally keeps to leather or studded leather to protect their skills; may use shields but gains no shield bash.
DwarfSapper.DwarfTraitsDesc: Infravision to 60'. Base movement speed 20'. Can smell gold and gems as a dwarf.
DwarfSapper.UndergroundSkillsDesc: Underground, adds class level to detect traps, slanting passages, shifting walls, and new construction, and is considered trained (d20) for engineering-related skill checks.
DwarfSapper.ThievingSkillsDesc: Backstab, sneak silently, hide in shadows, pick locks, and find/disable (and set) traps per the level table, plus one elective thief skill of the player's choice (not read magic scrolls). Needs thieves' tools, purchased at 1st level.
DwarfSapper.LuckAndWitsDesc: When expending Luck, rolls a luck die (per level) for each point spent and applies it to the check. Recovers lost Luck at a number of points equal to their level each week, up to the natural maximum.
DwarfSapper.AlchemicalLearningsDesc: Brews secret compounds from gathered reagents: lumigoo (a dim glow), boommush (a fire-triggered explosive), and dragonscat (an intense, even-underwater burn). Damage and cost scale with level; non-dwarves risk the charge going off in hand.
DwarfSapper.Credit: Dwarf Sapper by Keith Nelson — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2017 Collection, Volume 6: Men and Magic.
InvincibleChicken.WeaponTrainingDesc: Wields no weapons, but deals 1d4 damage from a flurry of pecks and claws.
InvincibleChicken.CommunicationDesc: Speaks eagle (per Appendix L). At the judge's discretion, one particularly dumb farmer (Int 6 or below) can understand the chicken's clucks — and only that farmer.
InvincibleChicken.MountedCombatDesc: While carried by a farmer who understands it, the chicken counts as mounted on a trained mount; if the farmer is spooked (takes half its current hp), the chicken's riding roll drops to a d10.
InvincibleChicken.ArmorDesc: Wears no armor; instead starts at AC 12 and gains +1 AC per level as it learns to dodge.
InvincibleChicken.SmallSizeDesc: A bit larger than a normal chicken but still small — can crawl into narrow passages and through tiny holes.
InvincibleChicken.SandersBlessingDesc: In an inhospitable environment, receives a +2 bonus to Fortitude saves.
InvincibleChicken.StealthDesc: Sneaks and hides well, gaining the Sneak & Hide bonus from the table, used as the thief's abilities.
InvincibleChicken.FowlLuckDesc: Once per session, at the cost of 1 Luck, may force the judge to re-roll any roll; the new result stands, better or worse.
InvincibleChicken.LimitedFlightDesc: As a full-round action may run, flap, and land — up to 30' (15' airborne), no higher than 5'. Falling damage is reduced by 1d6.
InvincibleChicken.HeadlessActionsDesc: If (and only if) the chicken's head is cut off, it can still take 1d4 actions after the head is gone.
InvincibleChicken.Credit: The Invincible Chicken by Randy Andrews, art by Jaiden Bornt — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2017 Collection, Volume 6: Men and Magic.
Orc.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained in the battleaxe, club, dagger, spear, two-handed sword, shortbow, sling, and warhammer, preferring two-handed weapons. May wear any armor made for them (usually only leather). Orc tribes view shields as cowardly — never used.
Orc.RageDieAbilityDesc: Rolls a rage die with each attack in place of a fixed attack bonus, applying it to both attack and damage rolls.
Orc.RageburnDesc: Before rolling to attack, may expend Personality or Intelligence: each point gives +1 to hit, and a landed hit becomes an automatic critical. May also rageburn after a crit to add or subtract the rage die from the crit result. A point of Per/Int recovers each day no rageburn is used. A natural 1 after declaring rageburn permanently loses an expended point.
Orc.FerocityDesc: When reduced to 0 or fewer hit points, may take one final attack action before falling, resolved immediately after the damage is dealt.
Orc.InfravisionDesc: Infravision to 60', but poor in daylight: -1 to attack rolls and skill checks that rely on precise vision.
Orc.LuckDesc: The Luck modifier applies to Fortitude saving throws.
Orc.LanguagesDesc: Speaks Orcish; leaders or exceptional orcs living near humans also speak Common.
Orc.Credit: Orc by Andrew Sternick — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2017 Collection, Volume 6: Men and Magic.
HalfOrc.WeaponTrainingDesc: Half-orcs raised among humans are trained in the battleaxe, club, dagger, flail, handaxe, javelin, longbow, longsword, mace, polearm, shortbow, short sword, sling, spear, staff, two-handed sword, and warhammer, and may wear any armor; those raised among orcs use the orc weapon set. Half-orcs never use shields.
HalfOrc.RageDieAbilityDesc: Rolls a rage die with each attack in place of a fixed attack bonus, applying it to both attack and damage rolls.
HalfOrc.RageburnDesc: Before rolling to attack, may expend Personality or Intelligence: each point gives +1 to hit, and a landed hit becomes an automatic critical. May also rageburn after a crit to add or subtract the rage die from the crit result. A point of Per/Int recovers each day no rageburn is used. A natural 1 after declaring rageburn permanently loses an expended point.
HalfOrc.FerocityDesc: When reduced to 0 or fewer hit points, may take one final action — used in any way, not just an attack — resolved immediately after the damage is dealt.
HalfOrc.InfravisionDesc: Infravision to 30' and sees normally in daylight.
HalfOrc.LuckDesc: The Luck modifier applies to Fortitude saving throws.
HalfOrc.Credit: Half-Orc by Andrew Sternick — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2017 Collection, Volume 6: Men and Magic.
PaladinGambrinus.WeaponTrainingDesc: Trained in the holy weapons of Gambrinus — the threshing flail (1d6), harvest sickle (1d4, or 1d8 as a two-handed scythe), and bungstarter of peace (1d4+1) — plus the battleaxe, club, dagger, handaxe, longsword, mace, short sword, two-handed sword, warhammer, longbow, shortbow, crossbow, and sling. May wear any armor with no spell-check penalty, and uses shields. Always carries a never-empty holy cask and a vessel for the sacrament.
PaladinGambrinus.SmiteDesc: May empower their bungstarter, flail, or sickle with divine power, adding the smite die to attack and damage rolls in place of the normal attack bonus. Smite is limited to those Gambrinus deems unholy — defilers of hops and grains, teetotalers, and those who would destroy the sacred wort-tuns.
PaladinGambrinus.MagicDesc: Casts cleric spells from the Gambrinus list using the Personality modifier, with cleric-style disapproval that can be reduced through sacrifice.
PaladinGambrinus.HolyDeedsDesc: Quaffs a beer and rolls action die + paladin level + Personality + Luck on the holy deeds table to improve battle prowess, aid companions, heal, or invoke Gambrinus. Each failure increases disapproval, and drinking risks drunkenness.
PaladinGambrinus.LayOnHandsDesc: Offers a drink from the holy cask and concentrates for 1 action to heal the faithful, as the cleric's lay on hands (one drink per HD healed).
PaladinGambrinus.InstillBraveryDesc: Grants allies who drink from the holy cask a bonus to saves versus fear (from dragons, outsiders, undead, and mundane sources) for 2 minutes + 1 minute/level; bonus and number of targets per the holy deeds table.
PaladinGambrinus.CauseFearDesc: Causes the unholy of equal HD or less to make a Will save or flee for 1 round + 1 round/level; number of targets per the holy deeds table.
PaladinGambrinus.DrunkennessDesc: Gambrinus' brew is potent: each drink in a day requires a Fortitude save (DC 5 + 2 per drink that day; the smite die may aid it). A failed save imposes -1d on the dice chain to all further actions that day, cumulative, cleared by a good sleep.
PaladinGambrinus.CasterNote: Cleric spells are managed on the Spells tab and use Personality. Spells known rise from 0 (level 1) to 6 (level 10); max spell level from 0 to 3. Gambrinus' spell list is given in the class writeup.
PaladinGambrinus.Credit: Paladin of Gambrinus by Keith Nelson — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2017 Collection, Volume 6: Men and Magic.
BloodyHound.HalflingBaseDesc: A hard-boiled P.I. for Nowhere City Nights, a bloody hound is a halfling in all ways except those below — and full-size rather than a half-man. Uses the halfling class table for attack, crit, saves, and Sneak & Hide. Gains XP by discovering clues, making deductions, and finding answers rather than by killing.
BloodyHound.MultiTaskerDesc: Doing two things at once: uses two 1d16 action dice each round, fighting or not. But scores a critical hit only when both dice attack the same opponent, one comes up a natural 16, and the other roll also hits.
BloodyHound.NightOwlDesc: Can see 30' in near-total darkness (stray window light, a crescent moon). No benefit in pitch black, when blindfolded, or against the invisible.
BloodyHound.DetectiveDesc: Trained in all six investigation skills (regardless of background) plus a unique seventh, Follow a Hunch (Luck). Auto-succeeds at finding 'obvious' clues, adds class level to investigation checks, and can 'dog a case' — a Luck-burn/spellburn hybrid burned after the roll.
BloodyHound.HardBoiledDesc: Gains 2 bonus points per point of Luck burned (1 if no ally is within 120') and may instead burn Luck to reduce incoming damage 1-for-1. Luck restores nightly at a rate equal to their level. Not a halfling good-luck charm: each Luck burn temporarily drains 1 Luck from a chosen ally within 120'.
BloodyHound.ObsessionsDesc: The Truth is too big to grasp whole, so each bloody hound fixates on one piece of it — an obsession (roll 1d14) that drives their truth-seeking no matter the cost.
BloodyHound.Credit: Bloody Hound by Steven Bean — The Gongfarmer's Almanac 2017 Collection, Volume 7: Adventures and Settings (for Nowhere City Nights).
